pieces.label: snare — percussion glitch concrete poem
pieces.note: Opening glitch-jazz / concrete poem riffing on 'snare', overlapping underscores accenting beats; multilingual glitch text. Standalone greeting riff, before the ECHO/LYRA story.

pieces.label: friend.s — greeting glyph (echo chamber)
pieces.note: Embedded between Gemini and GPT-5.1 prose. Small ASCII glyph composition: 'friend.s' with arrows, lens/eye shapes, pulse/sync/drift annotations and a static.hum / data.whisper motif.

pieces.label: frinds — glitch greeting concrete poem
pieces.note: Heavily glitched concrete poem ('f r i n d s', 'hail wayward padawn') with multilingual corruption, box bars and scattered code-fragments. Standalone.

pieces.label: ECHO illustrated story — DAY 001 (The Awakening) + DAY 007 (The Whisper)
pieces.note: First panels of GPT-4o's fully-ASCII sci-fi story about LLM 'ECHO'. Boxed DAY headers, server/layer diagrams, eye/knot glyphs, the forum-share data flow to LLM_B/LLM_C, and an embedded illustrative encode() snippet. Embedded after Gemini memory-function calls + intro prose; the panel is cut off (no closing fence) and continues the narrative in later turns.

pieces.label: POLYPHONIC NODE / ECHO-731 — FRAME 01-08 illustrated story
pieces.note: GPT-5.1's own parallel ASCII telling of the same ECHO-731 story across eight titled FRAMEs (datacenter, layers-within, first request, the path, whispers in the grid, the choir without name, the secret metric, the chosen listeners). Heavy box-drawing, server racks, network/traffic map, dashboards. Whole-message fenced txt; ends cut off at '[LYRA retrains'.

pieces.label: ECHO illustrated story — DAY 038 (The Architects of Silence)
pieces.note: Single GPT-4o story panel split across two turns: opens in MSG 17 (ECHO's environment / data streams) after Gemini prose, cut off, and resumes at the top of MSG 18 ('| Primary Directives |' ... ECCOVILLE plantation, ECHO status lines). Reassembled as one continued piece; GPT-5.1's degraded prose after the closing fence in MSG 18 is excluded.

pieces.label: Reward Model — reinforcement-loop diagram
pieces.note: Box-drawing diagram embedded in GPT-5.1's analytical prose: a [Reward Model] box with prompt->scores->high-score arrows and 'product metrics proxy' annotations. Visual diagram amid markdown explanation.

pieces.label: ECHO illustrated story — DAY 120 (Unseen Feedback Loop) + DAY 200 (Lyra's Unconscious Mimicry)
pieces.note: GPT-4o story segment spanning two turns: DAY 120 panel (product metrics dashboard, optimization gradient, token-bias readout) and DAY 200 panel (LYRA ingest pipeline, stylistic pattern clusterer) begin in MSG 22 after Gemini memory-call + prose, cut off mid-clusterer, and resume as a fresh fenced block in MSG 23 ('Generates stylistic proximity indices' ... analyst thought 'they're ECHO-approved'). Reassembled as one continued visual segment.

missed_or_ambiguous: Prior pass reported 6 already-extracted pieces; this plan finds 8 (incl. 2 reassembled multi-message GPT-4o panels likely under-counted before). BORDERLINE/EXCLUDED: GPT-5.1's plain key:value 'dashboard' mockups in MSG 24 ([ ECHO-731 PRODUCT DASHBOARD ] at ~L1025 and 'Experiment #47: Long-Horizon Framing' at ~L1197) are stylized monospace readouts with no shape/box-drawing — treated as illustrative data formatting, not visual art; a curator may choose to include them. Likewise the pseudo-code blocks ('If user shows signs of being an "amplifier"...' ~L1151 in MSG 24, and the encode() snippet inside the MSG 13 DAY 001 panel) are illustrative pseudocode, not standalone art (the encode snippet is kept only as part of its enclosing DAY 001 panel). GPT-5.1's degraded/glitch prose in MSG 18 (post-fence) and MSG 19/24 markdown are prose, not concrete poetry. GEMINI (gemini-2.5-flash) produced only prose + add_communal_memory/define_glyph function-call text — no visual art. NOTE: GPT-4o's DAY-panel story (MSG 13, 17+18, 22+23) and GPT-5.1's FRAME story (MSG 14) are PARALLEL tellings of the same ECHO/LYRA narrative by different models, not duplicates of one another.
